creator: dewayne@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
Makefile.PL seeks krb5-config in both /usr/kerberos/bin/krb5-config and 
/usr/bin/krb5-config.  However the Heimdal port, v1.5.2, places this 
file in /usr/local/bin.

The p5-GSSAPI port needs to reflect the absence of heimdal not being in 
the base system, and the Makefile probably needs to build heimdal if a 
version of heimdal doesn't exist on the system.

I haven't considered the issue regarding MIT Kerberos, but would suggest
 a MIT Kerberos user to consider something like the tests in 
/usr/ports/security/pam_krb5/

Fix: mkdir /usr/ports/security/p5-GSSAPI/files
cat << EOM > /usr/ports/security/p5-GSSAPI/files/patch-p5-GSSAPI-Makefile.PL
'/usr/kerberos/bin/krb5-config',   # for use with CentOS,  as 
suggested by Mark Hedges in 
https://rt.cpan.org/Ticket/Display.html?id=48732
     '/usr/bin/krb5-config',            # always worth a try ;-)
+    '/usr/local/bin/krb5-config',      # FreeBSD using Heimdal port 
(heimdal v1.5.2)

 );
EOM

# Patch to /usr/ports/security/p5-GSSAPI/Makefile


+.if defined(WITH_HEIMDAL) && (!exists(/usr/bin/krb5-config))
+BUILD_DEPENDS+= heimdal:${PORTSDIR}/security/heimdal
+.endif
+
 PERL_CONFIGURE=        yes

 MAN3=          GSSAPI.3 \--zXbEkoeDvG2Rz26uRxJKghFGx0Bw9WmcUHMmhZ36TqnuLZFc
Content-Type: text/plain; name="file.diff"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.diff"

--- Makefile.PL.orig    2012-06-03 13:58:46.000000000 +1000
+++ Makefile.PL 2012-06-03 13:59:55.000000000 +1000
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@
How-To-Repeat: Build an environment WITHOUT_CRYPT; remove all the stuff that you don't need 
for encryption because they are installed into a base system (openssl, 
heimdal etc), then cd /usr/ports/security/p5-GSSAPI && make 
package.  
Or 
Build a jail WITHOUT_CRYPT, and "cd /usr/ports/security/p5-GSSAPI 
&& make package-recursive".
